SEARCH
Package io.perl.logger

Interface PerformanceLogger

All Superinterfaces:
Print, ReportLatency
All Known Implementing Classes:
DefaultLogger, DefaultPrometheusLogger, ResultsLogger
public interface PerformanceLogger extends Print, ReportLatency
High-level logger interface used by the PerL framework to report benchmarking metrics. Implementations receive periodic summaries and a final total summary and may also be asked to record per-event latency information via ReportLatency.

Important contract points:

  • Latency values are expressed in the time unit returned by getTimeUnit() (default is TimeUnit.ms).
  • Implementations should avoid blocking the PerL runtime. If a logging implementation performs I/O (network or disk), prefer asynchronous or batched writes.
  • The arrays passed for percentiles are parallel: values in percentileLatencies match indices in percentileLatencyCounts if provided.
  • Implementations should be thread-safe if shared across threads, or documented otherwise. For example, PerlChannel instances are not thread-safe and should not be shared between threads.

Typical usage example: 

 
 PerformanceLogger logger = new DefaultLogger();
 // PerlBuilder will accept the logger when building a Perl instance
 Perl perl = PerlBuilder.build(logger, null, null, null);

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default long
    getMaxLatency()
    Maximum latency value considered valid by the logger (default from LatencyConfig).
    default long
    getMinLatency()
    Minimum latency value considered valid by the logger (default from LatencyConfig).
    default double[]
    getPercentiles()
    Percentile fractions (for example 0.5 for the 50th percentile) that this logger expects to receive.
    default int
    getPrintingIntervalSeconds()
    Reporting interval in seconds (default from PerlConfig).
    default TimeUnit
    getTimeUnit()
    Time unit used to express latencies by this logger.
    void
    printTotal(double seconds, long bytes, long records, double recsPerSec, double mbPerSec, double avgLatency, long minLatency, long maxLatency, long invalid, long lowerDiscard, long higherDiscard, long slc1, long slc2, long[] percentileLatencies, long[] percentileLatencyCounts)
    Print the Periodic performance results.
    default void
    recordLatency(long startTime, int events, int bytes, long latency)
    Default no-op implementation to record per-event latency.

    Methods inherited from interface io.perl.logger.Print

    print

  • Method Details

    • printTotal

      void printTotal(double seconds, long bytes, long records, double recsPerSec, double mbPerSec, double avgLatency, long minLatency, long maxLatency, long invalid, long lowerDiscard, long higherDiscard, long slc1, long slc2, long[] percentileLatencies, long[] percentileLatencyCounts)
      Print the Periodic performance results.

      This method is invoked periodically by the PerL framework to present interval-level metrics. Implementations must interpret latency-related parameters in the time unit returned by getTimeUnit().

      Parameters:
      seconds - Reporting duration in seconds
      bytes - Number of bytes read/write
      records - Number of records processed in the interval
      recsPerSec - Records per second
      mbPerSec - Throughput in MB per second
      avgLatency - Average latency (in getTimeUnit())
      minLatency - Minimum latency (in getTimeUnit())
      maxLatency - Maximum latency (in getTimeUnit())
      invalid - Count of invalid/negative latency records
      lowerDiscard - Count of latencies below configured minimum
      higherDiscard - Count of latencies above configured maximum
      slc1 - Sliding latency coverage metric (implementation-defined)
      slc2 - Sliding latency coverage metric (implementation-defined)
      percentileLatencies - Array holding latency values for configured percentiles
      percentileLatencyCounts - Parallel array of counts for each percentile bucket (may be null)

    • recordLatency

      default void recordLatency(long startTime, int events, int bytes, long latency)
      Default no-op implementation to record per-event latency. Implementations can override to collect more detailed latency information.
      Specified by:
      recordLatency in interface ReportLatency
      Parameters:
      startTime - event start time (time unit depends on caller)
      events - number of events included in this record
      bytes - bytes processed for the events
      latency - measured latency for the event(s)

    • getPrintingIntervalSeconds

      default int getPrintingIntervalSeconds()
      Reporting interval in seconds (default from PerlConfig).
      Returns:
      reporting interval in seconds

    • getTimeUnit

      default TimeUnit getTimeUnit()
      Time unit used to express latencies by this logger. Default is TimeUnit.ms.
      Returns:
      time unit for latencies

    • getMinLatency

      default long getMinLatency()
      Minimum latency value considered valid by the logger (default from LatencyConfig). Values below this are treated as discarded.
      Returns:
      minimum valid latency

    • getMaxLatency

      default long getMaxLatency()
      Maximum latency value considered valid by the logger (default from LatencyConfig). Values above this are treated as discarded.
      Returns:
      maximum valid latency

    • getPercentiles

      default double[] getPercentiles()
      Percentile fractions (for example 0.5 for the 50th percentile) that this logger expects to receive. The PerL framework will compute percentile latency values using the configured fractions and pass them back in print calls.
      Returns:
      array of percentile fractions
